action.reasoning:
Observation: After resuming for 25 more epochs (epochs 25-49), best val exprate climbed to 0.0649 at epoch 42 (previously 0.0609). Loss declined steadily 1.46→1.31. Test exprate was 0.0509 from earlier eval. Time: 1.59h, 10 iters left. The model is improving slowly - 0.4% over 25 epochs. Submitting the new best checkpoint should give a modest improvement.

Analysis: Training is making slow progress. Rather than train more epochs (which will give marginal gains), let me submit the current best checkpoint (which is now at val exprate 0.0649 from ep 42) to lock in the improvement. Then continue training in background if time permits.

Decision: Submit eval with current best.pt to lock in improvement, then continue training in next iter.
action.command: ls -la checkpoints/ && python -c "import torch; ck=torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt', map_location='cpu'); print('best.pt epoch', ck.get('epoch'), 'best', ck.get('best'))"
